workingTreeContext: The focused tests were run after adding semantic-fallback and action-bucket breakdown assertions; this evidence bundle, review result, regenerated claim artifacts, and matching test changes are intended to be committed together.
summary: Focused deterministic tests prove adapter-owned runner dispatch, fixture runtime proof-class downgrade, portable semantic-group fallback, refresh-plan coordinator summary fields, action-bucket status breakdowns, and read-only runner readiness/status packet composition.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: deterministic CLI, packet, adapter, and runner-readiness contracts
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: This bundle proves the named packet and command-routing contracts through focused unit and CLI smoke tests. It does not prove semantic quality of arbitrary host runners, model behavior, or app-specific correctness.
notClaimed: Every consumer adapter command is honest
Every omitted semantic group is product-meaningful
Every refresh-plan consumer reads the summary correctly
The broader blocked split-field agent-reading claim

protectedBehaviors: adapter claim discovery entries, depth, state path, audience hints, and semantic groups are applied
omitted semantic groups fall back to `General product behavior`
refresh-plan packets expose `refreshSummary` status, coordinator-facing summary text, counts, changed sources, and next actions
claim status action buckets include `byReviewStatus` and `byEvidenceStatus` breakdowns
agent status includes runner readiness before claim branches
doctor and agent status share runner readiness facts
typed runner readiness resolves per-surface command templates and smoke branches
`eval test` selects an adapter-declared typed runner by surface and executes its command template
`agent status` returns read-only no-input orientation with binary, skill, adapter, claim-state, and scan-scope fields
fixture runtime writes observed proof with `proofClass=fixture-smoke` and `proofClassSource=runtime`
fixture runtime preserves adapter-declared proof class only as declared metadata

claimSupport.reason: `TestCLIEvalTestSelectsTypedRunnerBySurface` and typed runner readiness tests prove command templates are loaded from adapter-owned runner declarations rather than hardcoded benchmark runners.

`TestCLIEvalTestAcceptsFixtureRuntime` and `TestCLIEvalTestSelectsTypedRunnerBySurface` prove fixture runtime downgrades observed proof to `fixture-smoke` while preserving declared proof metadata separately.
`TestDiscoverClaimProofPlanUsesPortableSemanticFallbackWhenAdapterOmitsGroups` proves omitted adapter semantic groups produce `General product behavior`.
The same semantic fallback test proves the binary uses the portable fallback group when adapter semantic groups are omitted.
`TestBuildClaimRefreshPlanMarksChangedSources` proves refresh-plan packets include coordinator-facing summary status, non-mutation summary text, counts, changed sources, and next actions.
`TestBuildClaimStatusSummarySummarizesExistingPacket` proves every primary action bucket includes `byReviewStatus` and `byEvidenceStatus` counts.
`TestCLIEvalTestSelectsTypedRunnerBySurface` proves `eval test` runs bounded evaluations through adapter-declared runner command templates.

`TestRunAgentStatusJSONReturnsNoInputOrientation`, `TestAgentStatusIncludesRunnerReadinessBranchBeforeClaimBranches`, and `TestDoctorAndAgentStatusShareRunnerReadinessFacts` cover the read-only status packet composition including binary, skill, adapter, claim, and runner readiness surfaces.

decision.reason: The focused deterministic tests directly cover the named binary, packet, adapter, and runner-readiness behaviors without needing model or Cautilus eval fixture evidence.
